name: Cow's Ice Cream
note: Canada's best ice cream — worth the drive to Whistler Village.

name: BeaverTails
note: Hot pastry on a cold mountain. The classic après-ski move.

name: La Casa Gelato
note: Over 200 flavours at any given time. Decision paralysis guaranteed.

name: Ricardo's
note: Robson's go-to candy and gelato spot. Dangerous after dinner.

name: Dairy Queen
note: Kensington Square DQ. Blizzards hit different at this one.

name: Ladurée
note: Parisian macarons on Robson. Feels fancy, tastes fancier.

name: The Waffle Co
note: Indian-style waffles and desserts. The loaded ones are unreal.

name: Jinya Ramen
note: Tonkotsu done right. The spicy creamy vegan is a sleeper hit.

name: Mr. Shawarma
note: Best shawarma in downtown. The garlic sauce is non-negotiable.

name: Sula Indian Restaurant
note: Proper Indian food on the Drive. The butter chicken is legit.

name: Old Spaghetti Factory
note: Gastown institution. Unlimited bread and nostalgia.

name: Chongqing Restaurant
note: Sichuan heat near Metrotown. Order the boiled fish if you dare.

name: Manjal South Indian Kitchen
note: South Indian comfort food. The dosas are massive.

name: Din Tai Fung
note: The soup dumplings live up to the hype. Expect a wait.

name: Vij's
note: Vikram Vij's legendary spot. No reservations, always worth the wait.

name: Chicken World
note: Late-night fried chicken near English Bay. Simple and perfect.

name: Uncle Fatih's Pizza
note: SFU campus staple. The pide slices fuel every study session.
